nodes.label: AI as idea generation and task breakdown partner
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI for idea generation mainly. Sometimes I have a starting idea, but not sure where to go next

nodes.label: AI as error checker and rewriter
nodes.grounding_span: ask AI to rewrite things for me sometimes, and to check for any errors

nodes.label: AI adherence to specific instructions
nodes.grounding_span: I get frustrated when I ask it to NOT do something, but it does it anyway

nodes.label: AI use in academia vs. other roles
nodes.grounding_span: in academia, yes, because it is frowned upon, so I do not use it. In my other role, I use it freely

nodes.label: Preserving personal cognitive skills
nodes.label_negative: Over-reliance on AI leading to skill loss
nodes.grounding_span: I get worried that I will become reliant on AI and not be able to use my own thinking

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner vs. delegator
nodes.grounding_span: I am more likely to collaborate, and generate ideas then go and do the tasks it suggests myself

nodes.label: Productivity and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: satisfied when I am given a workaround that saves me hours

nodes.label: Cognitive autonomy and skill maintenance
nodes.grounding_span: I try to make sure I do things myself so that I don't forget the skills

nodes.label: Authenticity and personal connection to work
nodes.grounding_span: more deeply connected with the content, and have it committed to memory

nodes.label: Compliance with workplace norms
nodes.grounding_span: academia is strict on AI use, so I am careful to follow rules

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for task breakdown and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: satisfied when I am given a workaround that saves me hours

nodes.label: Frustration with AI not following instructions
nodes.grounding_span: I get frustrated when I ask it to NOT do something, but it does it anyway

nodes.label: Concern about over-reliance and skill loss
nodes.grounding_span: I get worried that I will become reliant on AI and not be able to use my own thinking

nodes.label: Preference for collaboration over delegation
nodes.grounding_span: I am more likely to collaborate, and generate ideas then go and do the tasks it suggests myself

nodes.label: Optimism about future AI capabilities
nodes.grounding_span: I feel I will be able to delegate more tasks, and ask it to do more

nodes.label: Iterative strategic questioning — breaks problems into sub-questions
nodes.grounding_span: I will say something like 'I am thinking of designing a new product, but I don't know whether to niche it by design, or by function. What do you suggest?'

nodes.label: Domain-sensitive rule adherence — adjusts AI use based on context
nodes.grounding_span: in academia, yes, because it is frowned upon, so I do not use it. In my other role, I use it freely
